title: Neon Genesis Evangelion Classical Volume
html:
Contents

  • 1 Beethoven
  • 2 Verdi
  • 3 Handel
  • 4 J.S. Bach

Beethoven[edit]

"Neon Genesis Evangelion Classical Volume 1 - Beethoven" is a CD album containing a recording of Ludwig van Beethoven's Ninth Symphony, played by the Brussels Philharmonic and conducted by Alexander Rahbari. The fourth movement was used in Episode 24 of the series, and was previously released on Neon Genesis Evangelion Addition. It was released on October 22, 1997 by King Records with the catalog number KICC-231.

Neon Genesis Evangelion Classical Volume 1 - Beethoven
No. Title Length
1. "1st Movement: Allegro ma non troppo, un poco maestoso" 15:56
2. "2nd Movement: Molto vivace" 17:14
3. "3rd Movement: Adagio molto e cantabile" 10:56
4. "4th Movement: Presto" 23:04
Total length:66:30

Verdi[edit]

"Neon Genesis Evangelion Classical Volume 2 - Verdi" is a double-CD set containing a recording of Messa da Requiem by Giuseppe Verdi, played by the Slovak Radio New Philharmonic Orchestra and conducted by Alexander Rahbari. The piece "Dies irae" was used in the trailers for End of Evangelion and was previously released on Neon Genesis Evangelion Addition. The album was released on October 22, 1997 by King Records with the catalog number KICC-232.

Neon Genesis Evangelion Classical Volume 2 - Verdi (CD1)
No. Title Length
1. "Requiem" 7:37
2. "Dies irae" 37:07
Total length:44:44
Neon Genesis Evangelion Classical Volume 2 - Verdi (CD2)
No. Title Length
1. "Offertorium" 10:44
2. "Sanctus" 2:49
3. "Angus Dei" 4:53
4. "Lux aeterna" 5:55
5. "Libera me" 13:04
Total length:36:05

Handel[edit]

"Neon Genesis Evangelion Classical Volume 3 - Handel" is a two-CD album released on 22 November 1997 by King Records. It features pieces originally composed by George Frideric Handel, played by the Amor Artis Chorale & English Chamber Orchestra and conducted by Johannes Somary. It was released as one CD with catalog number KICC-234 and it peaked at 96 in the Oricon charts, making only one appearance.

J.S. Bach[edit]

"Neon Genesis Evangelion Classical Vol. 4 - J.S. Bach" is a compilation CD album containing Johann Sebastian Bach's Classical Pieces used in the theatrical feature. It was released in November 11, 2003, produced by King Records with catalog number KICC 236. The performance was conducted by Vassil Kazandjiev. Tracks 1-6 are of 'Suite No. 1 For Cello Solo In G Major', BWV. 1007, tracks 7-12 are of 'Partita No. 3 For Violin Solo In E Major', BWV. 1006, tracks 13-17 are of 'Orchestral Suite No. 3 In D Major', BWV. 1068, and track 18 is "Jesu, Joy Of Man's Desiring".
